name: Maine Coon
overview: One of the largest domesticated cat breeds and native to the US state of Maine, Maine Coons are known for their tufted ears, bushy tails, and dog-like sociability.

name: Siamese
overview: Originating in Thailand (formerly Siam), Siamese cats are known for their striking blue eyes, color-point coat, and famously vocal personality.

name: Persian
overview: Persians are among the oldest known cat breeds, prized for their long coat and flat face, which requires daily grooming and regular eye cleaning.

name: Ragdoll
overview: Developed in California in the 1960s, Ragdolls are known for going limp when picked up, which is how the breed got its name.

name: Bengal
overview: Bred from crosses with the Asian leopard cat, Bengals have a distinctive wild-looking spotted coat and much higher exercise needs than most domestic breeds.

name: British Shorthair
overview: One of the oldest English cat breeds, the British Shorthair is known for its dense, plush coat and calm, low-maintenance temperament.

name: Sphynx
overview: Best known for being hairless, the Sphynx actually requires more skin care than furred breeds -- regular bathing to remove oil buildup is essential.

name: Abyssinian
overview: One of the oldest known cat breeds, Abyssinians have a distinctive ticked coat and are among the most active and playful domestic cat breeds.

name: Scottish Fold
overview: Known for the folded-ear gene that gives the breed its name, Scottish Folds carry a cartilage condition that responsible breeders and owners should be aware of.

name: Russian Blue
overview: Believed to originate near Russia's Arkhangelsk region, Russian Blues have a dense silvery-blue coat and are known for being reserved with strangers but deeply loyal to family.
